title: Broad MLP Family Audit
research_question: Is the current relu^2 MLP actually the right activation family for the current frontier backbone, or should the lab move toward smoother or gated MLPs?

candidates.name: w1-relu2-control
candidates.goal: Establish a tranche-local control run on the new MLP code path.
candidates.hypothesis: The new MLP mode implementation should preserve the current relu^2 behavior cleanly.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether later activation-family comparisons are being made on a faithful control path.
candidates.why_worthy: A control is justified here because the MLP implementation surface itself changed.
candidates.description: run the current hybrid winner through the new relu^2 MLP path

candidates.name: w2-relu
candidates.goal: Test whether the square is doing meaningful work.
candidates.hypothesis: If polynomial emphasis matters, plain ReLU should lose to relu^2.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the frontier depends on the quadratic behavior rather than just positivity and sparsity.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the most basic mechanistic control inside the MLP family.
candidates.description: replace the current relu^2 MLP with plain ReLU

candidates.name: w3-silu
candidates.goal: Test a smooth modern activation family.
candidates.hypothesis: SiLU may give cleaner feature shaping than relu^2 on this small-model frontier.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the MLP family should pivot toward smooth nonlinearities.
candidates.why_worthy: This is one of the strongest broad-family alternatives to the current polynomial-style MLP.
candidates.description: replace the current MLP activation with SiLU

candidates.name: w4-gelu
candidates.goal: Test another smooth activation with softer gating behavior.
candidates.hypothesis: GELU may fit this small-model regime better than hard-threshold polynomial behavior.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the broad activation family should move toward GELU-like behavior.
candidates.why_worthy: This gives a true second smooth-family comparison rather than over-reading only SiLU.
candidates.description: replace the current MLP activation with GELU

candidates.name: w5-glu-silu
candidates.goal: Test whether gating is the real missing ingredient rather than just a different scalar activation.
candidates.hypothesis: A simple gated SiLU MLP may outperform all one-stream activations on this backbone.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the next MLP branch should become a gated-family branch rather than a polynomial-family branch.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest broad-family escalation beyond one-stream activations.
candidates.description: replace the current MLP with a simple gated SiLU variant
